project_overview.project_name: Innovation for Resilient Food Systems (Alianzas Rurales – PAR III) Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: Contribute to increased food security, market access and the adoption of climate-smart approaches by targeted project beneficiaries.
project_overview.total_financing: US$351.2 million (IBRD: US$300 million; Counterpart: US$51.2 million)
project_overview.implementation_period: 5 years implementation, 15 years capitalization (20-year evaluation period); Expected closing: November 30, 2027
project_overview.beneficiaries: 127,900 rural families across 2,560 subprojects; at least 1,000 RCOs (Rural Community Organizations) and 1,270 RPOs (Rural Producer Organizations); 290 complementary productive infrastructure subprojects; at least 30% women beneficiaries

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on Highlands, Amazon, and Sub-Andean regions
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Southern departments (Potosí, Chuquisaca, Tarija, Oruro, Santa Cruz) at moderately high risk of food insecurity; political and governance instability noted as substantial risk; no active armed conflict but institutional fragility and governance challenges present

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Capacity Building and Institutional Strengthening
key_components.description: Communication and dissemination strategy; formation and strengthening of RCOs and RPOs; capacity-building for technical service providers, municipalities, departments; technical assistance for subproject preparation and evaluation; policy dialogue support for food security expansion

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Support to Community and Productive Alliances
key_components.description: Community Alliance Subprojects (US$94.4M) for food security and vulnerability reduction; Productive Alliance Subprojects (US$101.8M) for market-oriented production with fixed capital investments and working capital; Complementary Productive Infrastructure Subprojects (US$113.0M) for public infrastructure including roads, bridges, irrigation, telecommunications, electricity
Equipment, consulting services, non-consulting services, operating costs for NCU and DOUs; project financial audits; technical studies; monitoring and evaluation; staffing for implementation support

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Rural Development and Land (MDRyT) through EMPODERAR's National Coordinating Unit (NCU); 11 Departmental Operating Units (DOUs) at decentralized level
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Operational Manual (POM) with detailed procedures; Subproject Agreements between EMPODERAR NCU and RCOs/RPOs/Municipalities/Departments; coordination with national and subnational governments; intergovernmental agreements for infrastructure maintenance; strong coordination within RCOs, RPOs and government levels critical to success
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: EMPODERAR NCU handles most procurement; RCOs and RPOs process procurement for Community and Productive Alliance Subprojects with NCU support; World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (November 2020); use of Government's integrated financial management system (SIGEP) and Treasury Single Account (TSA); Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) prepared; potential use of SOL application to simplify RCO/RPO procurement
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: EMPODERAR NCU M&E Unit coordinates data collection; Georeferenced Management Information System (SIGG) for real-time monitoring; baseline study in first year; mid-term review in year 3; final evaluation at project closing; quasi-experimental methodologies; impact evaluation at closing; semi-annual Progress Reports to World Bank; monitoring of climate co-benefits and GHG reductions in real-time

identified_risks.risk_description: Changes in authorities could lead to shifts in priorities and delays in decision-making; coordination challenges within central government and among government levels; institutional capacity constraints
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: WBG maintains dialogue with authorities at all levels; close implementation support and training in WBG fiduciary procedures and Anticorruption Guidelines; increased participation of Country Lawyer in key discussions; strong implementation track record of EMPODERAR NCU in PAR I and PAR II projects

identified_risks.risk_description: Globally constrained macroeconomic context may result in lower availability of counterpart financing; limited debt capacity of national and subnational governments; limited resources for operations and maintenance of infrastructure; high real exchange rate overvaluation (26-33%) hinders competitiveness
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: WBG will endeavor to provide 100% financing for proposed Project investments where feasible; Operations and Maintenance costs included in Project costs to ensure timely resource allocation and budgeting

identified_risks.risk_description: Inappropriate use of pesticides and contamination of soil/water; inadequate waste management from plastic inputs; displacement of native agrobiodiversity; degradation/fragmentation of natural habitats; substantial risk of underage child labor in family-based farming (467,874 children aged 5-14 in agriculture, 68% in agriculture); potential labor influx impacts from civil works; physical and economic displacement from minor civil works; loss of traditional indigenous knowledge; discrimination against indigenous minorities with itinerant production traditions; gender-based violence context (75% of women aged 15+ experienced physical/sexual violence)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF) with exclusion list prepared and disclosed; Resettlement Policy Framework prepared; Labor Management Plan with measures to prevent child and forced labor; Stakeholder Engagement Plan with grievance redress mechanism; Free, prior, and informed consent procedures for Indigenous Peoples; exclusion of subprojects in protected areas, with land-use change potential, involving new/large dams, or requiring land acquisition; workers' code of conduct based on Law 348; GBV prevention approach in GRM; mapping of GBV prevention institutions; consultation processes for displacement situations; adaptive management approach for ongoing risk assessment

identified_risks.risk_description: Weaknesses in public sector to attract and maintain qualified staff with high staff rotation; limited capacity of RCOs and RPOs for subproject implementation; need for enhanced procedures for complementary productive infrastructure subprojects (civil works)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Independent consultant firm contracted for selection and performance evaluation of Project staff (proven effective in PAR II); reliance on existing EMPODERAR NCU arrangements and expertise; adjustment of SIGG management information system to address Project needs; review and update of Operational Manual with enhanced procedures and tools; specific arrangements and mechanisms for participation, transparency and accountability throughout subproject cycle

identified_risks.risk_description: Staff rotation risks; disbursement of funds to RCOs and RPOs with limited capacity; payment processes for civil works and fraud/corruption risks; need for adequate internal controls and monitoring
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Independent consultant firm for staff selection and evaluation; use of SIGEP and TSA for disbursement processing; SIGG system for recording and monitoring physical and financial progress; simplified guidelines for RCOs/RPOs with record-keeping and documentation requirements; technical assistance for administrative tasks; clear roles and responsibilities with segregation of duties; alert-based mechanism in SIGG for timely identification of delays; participatory financial evaluation through 'rendición de cuentas'; counterpart contributions as incentive for sound management; annual independent audits by private firm acceptable to Bank; semi-annual interim financial reports

identified_risks.risk_description: Demand-driven subproject design with locations not known ex-ante; technology adoption rates uncertain; assumption that proposed technologies and practices will be attractive to farmers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF to be prepared during implementation with detailed screening and approval procedures; Environmental and Social Management Plans for each subproject; lessons learned from PAR I and PAR II incorporated; market orientation verification for productive alliances; participatory processes for subproject selection; technical assistance for subproject preparation; monitoring of adoption rates and adjustment mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Fixed exchange rate system limits macroeconomic tools; high real exchange rate overvaluation hinders competitiveness; fertilizer price volatility and supply constraints from Ukraine conflict; global food price spikes affecting vulnerable populations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project support to policy dialogue with MDRyT and other Ministries for food security expansion; focus on productivity improvements and climate resilience to reduce vulnerability; diversification of value chains; support for non-traditional exports (cocoa, beans, coffee, quinoa, Brazil nuts, beef)

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited participation of minoritarian indigenous groups and women in decision-making; cultural and social norms limiting women's participation; potential resistance to agricultural technology changes; need for effective communication with low-literacy populations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive Stakeholder Engagement Plan with stakeholder mapping and consultation processes; communication strategy with non-written tools for low-literacy populations; targeted trainings for women and indigenous peoples; sensitization of community leaders on gender and inclusion; grievance redress mechanism with 30-day response time; citizen engagement indicators in results framework; satisfaction surveys; participatory subproject selection and implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Bolivia ranks 10th globally in climate risk; exposure to droughts, floods, landslides; climate variability affecting rainfed agriculture; loss of mountain glaciers; projected temperature increases and rainfall changes; El Niño impacts; pest and disease distribution changes
identified_risks.risk_rating: moderate (overall climate/disaster risk to project outcomes)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Climate change and disaster risk screening completed; project design includes climate-smart agriculture technologies and practices; investments in irrigation and water management; soil conservation measures; climate-resilient crop varieties; improved livestock management; forest management for beekeeping; GHG mitigation benefits quantified (net reduction of 156.164 tCO2e/year); monitoring of climate co-benefits; adaptive management approach; technical assistance on climate adaptation

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (ESS1)
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (ESS3)
Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement (ESS5)
Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources (ESS6)
Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities (ESS7)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-level GRM with 30-day response time requirement; complaints tracked through SIGG information system; linkages to GBV prevention institutions; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; World Bank Accountability Mechanism (Inspection Panel and Dispute Resolution Service) available; community-level grievance mechanisms through RCOs and RPOs; public accounting ('rendición de cuentas') mechanisms for transparency and accountability
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan developed; target of 35% women farmers adopting improved technology; 35% of RPO business plan sales increases from women-led RPOs; 30% of technical service providers trained to be women; 30% of RCOs/RPOs formalized to be led by women; targeted assistance to women-led groups; women's participation in decision-making promoted through trainings and sensitization; communication strategy with non-written tools for low-literacy women; female technical service providers contracted; women's subprojects supported (agri-film/greenhouses, living fences, poultry houses, livestock sheds, crop storage, milking rooms, rainwater harvesting); satisfaction monitoring for women beneficiaries; recognition of gender gaps in income, labor market participation, credit access, education, and productive options
vulnerable_groups: Indigenous Peoples (78% of target population meet ESS7 criteria)
Families with school-age children (5-17 years) at risk of child labor
Indigenous minorities with itinerant production traditions

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed with conflict sensitivity considerations: addresses food insecurity as stress multiplier for migration; supports income generation and market access to reduce economic grievances; promotes inclusive participation of marginalized groups (indigenous peoples, women, youth) to enhance social cohesion; strengthens local institutions and governance capacity; includes grievance redress mechanisms to address community concerns; supports policy dialogue for food security expansion; focuses on resilience-building to reduce vulnerability to shocks; no activities in active conflict zones but operates in context of political instability and governance challenges
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Bolivia faces substantial political and governance risks with institutional fragility: changes in authorities affect priorities and implementation; coordination challenges between government levels; limited institutional capacity; high staff turnover in public sector; macroeconomic instability with limited fiscal space; high poverty and inequality (20.2% poverty in 2021, though declining); significant food insecurity (22.7% of households food insecure, 2.4 million undernourished); climate vulnerability (10th globally in climate risk); high dependence on agriculture for rural livelihoods; limited private sector investment (7% of GDP); 87% of private sector jobs informal; gender-based violence widespread (75% of women experienced physical/sexual violence); indigenous population marginalized (78% of target population); limited access to services and opportunities in rural areas
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: No direct security threats to project implementation mentioned; however, context of gender-based violence requires SEA/SH prevention measures in workers' code of conduct; labor influx from civil works requires monitoring for GBV risks; mapping of GBV prevention institutions required; linkages between GRM and GBV response institutions needed; child labor risks in family farming require monitoring and prevention measures; potential for discrimination against indigenous minorities requires safeguards; project operates in remote rural areas with limited state presence requiring decentralized implementation support
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No refugee populations mentioned; potential for minor physical and economic displacement from complementary productive infrastructure subprojects (civil works) expected to be low and manageable; displacement risks include: temporary/permanent economic displacement from minor civil works (walls, fences, small land portions, crops, fruit trees, livestock pasture, easements); potential minor physical displacement from on-site investments on privately-owned lands; Resettlement Policy Framework prepared to manage displacement risks; consultation processes required for all displacement situations; Free, prior, and informed consent procedures for Indigenous Peoples where applicable; Process Framework not required as no restrictions on access to natural resources anticipated; project may indirectly reduce displacement pressures by improving food security and income opportunities in rural areas
